items.headline: CBP Files Latest CAPE Progress Report: 85% Portal Complete
items.summary: CBP filed its March 31 status update showing the CAPE Claim Portal is now 85% complete, up from previous reports. The Mass Processing component remains limited to unliquidated entries and those within the 90-day voluntary reliquidation period. Judge Eaton continues to suspend the immediate refund order pending system completion.

items.headline: CIT Expands Refund Order to Include Finally Liquidated Entries
items.summary: Judge Eaton amended his March 20 order to include entries with final liquidations in IEEPA refund coverage. This means all entries subject to IEEPA duties are now entitled to refunds, regardless of their liquidation status or whether protests were filed within the 180-day window.

items.headline: Wave of Consumer Class Actions Target Tariff Pass-Through
items.summary: Major retailers including Costco, Fabletics, FedEx, and EssilorLuxottica face class action lawsuits alleging they passed IEEPA tariff costs to consumers but will retain government refunds. Plaintiffs seek restitution under unjust enrichment theories. Cases filed in multiple federal districts.

items.headline: Electronic ACH Refund Setup Critical as April Launch Nears
items.summary: CBP continues emphasizing that all IEEPA refunds will be electronic-only through ACH. Nearly 27,000 importers have registered for digital returns. Companies without ACH enrollment will have refund requests denied. CBP targeting late April 2026 for CAPE system launch.

items.headline: CAPE Form 4811 Enables Third-Party Refund Designations
items.summary: CBP clarifies that refunds can be issued to parties designated by importers using CBP Form 4811. This feature could be critical for tariff refund claim buyers to receive refunds directly for claims they have purchased, potentially creating a secondary market for refund rights.

items.headline: States Challenge Section 122 Replacement Tariffs as Illegal
items.summary: 24 states sued the Trump administration arguing the new 10% Section 122 replacement tariffs are also illegal. New York's AG stated the administration lacks authority for these tariffs. If successful, this could lead to additional refund opportunities beyond IEEPA duties.

items.headline: Senate Dems Introduce 'Tariff Refund Act of 2026'
items.summary: Senate Democrats introduced legislation requiring the administration to refund all IEEPA tariff revenue with interest within 180 days, prioritizing small businesses. The bill faces uncertain prospects given potential presidential veto and partisan divisions in Congress.

items.headline: Judge Eaton Designated Sole IEEPA Refund Case Handler
items.summary: CIT Chief Judge designated Judge Richard Eaton as the only judge to hear all IEEPA refund cases going forward. This consolidation aims to streamline the process for businesses seeking refunds and ensures consistent rulings across the estimated 2,000+ pending cases.
